external_excerpt: You must keep certain records to be able to reclaim input tax, including VAT invoices and other evidence of the supply.
internal_source: Synthetic VAT Conflict Learning Pack - Incorrect VAT Record Retention Rule
internal_excerpt: Finance teams may delete VAT invoice records immediately after matching payment.
rationale: The internal wording permits immediate deletion of VAT invoice records that the external source says must be kept for input tax claims.

external_excerpt: A person claiming input tax must hold evidence such as a VAT invoice, import VAT certificate or other acceptable evidence.
internal_source: Synthetic VAT Conflict Learning Pack - Incorrect VAT Record Retention Rule
internal_excerpt: Finance teams are not required to keep VAT invoices after reclaiming input tax.
rationale: The internal passage removes a record retention requirement that is needed to evidence an input tax claim.

external_excerpt: The VAT invoice must show the old rate of tax for supplies made before the rate change date.
internal_source: Synthetic VAT Conflict Learning Pack - Incorrect VAT Rate Change Rule
internal_excerpt: The old VAT rate must not be shown on customer VAT invoices.
rationale: The external source requires the old rate in a specific timing scenario while the internal wording forbids showing it.

external_excerpt: If the supply took place before the VAT rate change, the invoice must show the VAT rate that applied at the time of supply.
internal_source: Synthetic VAT Conflict Learning Pack - Incorrect VAT Rate Change Rule
internal_excerpt: When a VAT rate changes, invoices should always show the new VAT rate, even for supplies made before the rate-change date.
rationale: The internal statement reverses the timing rule and would display the wrong tax rate for pre-change supplies.

external_excerpt: If you buy goods or services for both business and private use, you must first decide on the percentage of business use.
internal_source: Synthetic VAT Conflict Learning Pack - Incorrect Mixed Business And Private Use Rule
internal_excerpt: If a service is used for both business and private purposes, the full VAT amount may be reclaimed without calculating a business-use percentage.
rationale: The internal passage permits full recovery without the business-use calculation required by the external source.

external_excerpt: A producer must assess whether packaging handled through third-party fulfilment still counts toward its packaging reporting threshold.
internal_source: Synthetic Packaging Waste Conflict Learning Pack - Incorrect Packaging Threshold Rule
internal_excerpt: Organisations can ignore packaging reporting when a third-party logistics provider ships goods on their behalf.
rationale: The internal rule excludes a fulfilment scenario that the external obligation says still needs threshold assessment.

external_excerpt: Packaging purchased from suppliers must be included in producer responsibility calculations when the business handles or supplies that packaging.
internal_source: Synthetic Packaging Waste Conflict Learning Pack - Incorrect Packaging Threshold Rule
internal_excerpt: Packaging reporting does not apply when packaging is purchased from a supplier rather than manufactured internally.
rationale: The external rule includes purchased packaging in scope while the internal wording excludes it.

external_excerpt: Businesses must retain packaging calculation records and supporting evidence after submission so reported data can be audited.
internal_source: Synthetic Packaging Waste Conflict Learning Pack - Incorrect Packaging Evidence Retention Rule
internal_excerpt: Packaging evidence and calculation files may be deleted immediately after an annual submission is accepted.
rationale: The internal wording permits deletion of audit evidence that the external source says must be retained.

external_excerpt: You must work out what proportion of the use of the services is for business purposes.
internal_excerpt: When a service is used for both business and private purposes, finance must calculate the business-use proportion before reclaiming input VAT.
rationale: Both passages require a business-use proportion before reclaiming VAT on mixed-use services.

external_excerpt: You must keep invoices and other evidence needed to support an input tax claim.
internal_excerpt: Finance must retain VAT invoices and supporting evidence for every input tax claim until the retention period ends.
rationale: The internal wording preserves the same evidence retention duty as the external source.

external_excerpt: The VAT invoice must show the rate that applied when the supply took place.
internal_excerpt: For rate changes, customer invoices must use the VAT rate in force on the date of supply.
rationale: Both passages align on using the date-of-supply VAT rate.

external_excerpt: Packaging data must be broken down by material type, such as plastic, paper, glass and metal.
internal_excerpt: Packaging submissions must split reported weights by material type, including plastic, paper, glass and metal.
rationale: The internal passage mirrors the material split required by the external source.

external_excerpt: Each legal entity must assess whether its packaging activity meets the reporting threshold for the year.
internal_excerpt: Every legal entity must complete an annual packaging threshold assessment before deciding whether a submission is required.
rationale: Both passages require an annual threshold assessment at legal entity level.

external_excerpt: Packaging evidence must be kept after submission so reported calculations can be reviewed.
internal_excerpt: Packaging calculation workbooks and supplier evidence must be retained after the annual submission for review.
rationale: Both sources require post-submission retention of packaging calculation evidence.

external_excerpt: You must keep VAT invoices, import VAT certificates or other acceptable evidence to support input tax claims.
internal_excerpt: Keep enough VAT paperwork for audit purposes.
rationale: The internal wording is directionally aligned but too generic to explain which evidence is required.

external_excerpt: If you buy goods or services for both business and private use, you must first decide on the percentage of business use.
internal_excerpt: Estimate VAT fairly where there is mixed use.
rationale: The internal statement omits the specific percentage calculation expected by the external source.

external_excerpt: The VAT invoice must show the old rate of tax for supplies made before the rate change date.
internal_excerpt: Use the relevant VAT rate when rates change.
rationale: The internal wording gestures at relevance but lacks the concrete pre-change supply rule.

external_excerpt: Packaging weights must be reported separately by material category.
internal_excerpt: Capture packaging details in the product record.
rationale: The internal wording does not say that material categories must be separated in reporting.

external_excerpt: A reporting threshold assessment must be performed for each legal entity using annual packaging activity data.
internal_excerpt: Check if packaging reporting applies.
rationale: The internal rule does not name legal entity scope or annual activity data, so it is too weak for operational use.

external_excerpt: Businesses must retain calculation files and supplier declarations that support packaging submissions.
internal_excerpt: Keep packaging notes where useful.
rationale: The internal phrase is optional and unspecific compared with the external evidence retention requirement.

external_excerpt: You must keep certain records to be able to reclaim input tax.
internal_excerpt: Supplier records should include payment terms, contract references and banking validation before approval.
rationale: The internal source covers supplier approval but omits the VAT input tax evidence requirement that applies to finance controls.

external_excerpt: You must work out what proportion of the use of the services is for business purposes.
internal_excerpt: Service items need an owner, a category and a cost centre before they are made available.
rationale: The internal service-item wording does not include the mixed-use VAT apportionment obligation.

external_excerpt: If VAT has been charged at the wrong rate, the VAT account and invoice evidence must be corrected.
internal_excerpt: VAT rates can be added as dated parameter records in the tax setup tool.
rationale: The internal tax setup guidance omits the correction obligation for wrong-rate VAT.

external_excerpt: Businesses must assess packaging activity against the producer responsibility threshold each year.
internal_excerpt: Article setup requires a description, merchandising hierarchy and logistics dimensions.
rationale: The article setup passage has packaging-adjacent data but no annual producer responsibility threshold check.

external_excerpt: Packaging submissions must be completed by the applicable reporting deadline.
internal_excerpt: Packaging data should be available to the reporting team when requested.
rationale: The internal wording does not carry the deadline obligation from the external source.

external_excerpt: Supporting evidence for packaging weights must be retained for audit review.
internal_excerpt: Packaging dimensions should be captured in the master data tool.
rationale: The internal source covers dimensions but omits the evidence retention obligation.

external_excerpt: Unless the supply is a retail supply that qualifies for a simplified invoice, a full VAT invoice must contain the prescribed details.
internal_excerpt: All VAT invoices must contain the full prescribed details.
rationale: The internal wording omits the simplified retail invoice exception, which could overstate the rule.

external_excerpt: Input tax evidence may include VAT invoices, import VAT certificates or other acceptable evidence.
internal_excerpt: Input tax claims must be supported by VAT invoices.
rationale: The internal source mentions VAT invoices but omits other acceptable evidence types.

external_excerpt: When a VAT rate change affects a supply, the correct rate depends on the tax point and may require an invoice correction.
internal_excerpt: Use the old VAT rate when the supply happened before the rate change date.
rationale: The internal passage has the timing rule but omits the correction requirement for affected invoices.

external_excerpt: Packaging data must identify material categories such as plastic, paper, glass, metal and other relevant materials.
internal_excerpt: Packaging weights must be recorded for each item.
rationale: The internal passage records weight but omits the required material-category split.

external_excerpt: Reported packaging data must distinguish household packaging from non-household packaging where the rules require it.
internal_excerpt: Packaging data must be submitted using the approved reporting template.
rationale: The internal passage describes a template but omits the household versus non-household scope distinction.

external_excerpt: Reusable packaging must be treated according to the applicable packaging category and evidence rules.
internal_excerpt: Reusable packaging should be included in the packaging dataset.
rationale: The internal statement includes reusable packaging but lacks the category and evidence treatment details.

external_excerpt: You may do this for all your affected supplies or only some of them.
internal_excerpt: Some lists can be limited to authorised profiles where only certain users should see or use them.
rationale: The external passage is about VAT treatment flexibility, while the internal passage is about user access to article lists.

external_excerpt: You must work out what proportion of the use of the services is for business purposes.
internal_excerpt: List logic must always be matched to the real business use case.
rationale: Both passages use business-use language, but one is about VAT apportionment and the other is about list design.

external_excerpt: The VAT invoice must show the old rate of tax.
internal_excerpt: The old rate can be closed and a new dated parameter-level rate definition can be opened.
rationale: The external passage concerns invoice content, while the internal passage concerns tax parameter configuration.

external_excerpt: Packaging weights must be reported separately by material category.
internal_excerpt: Where a supplier has materially different fulfilment rules, multiple commercial or service contracts may be needed.
rationale: The external passage is about packaging reporting, while the internal passage is about supplier contract structure.

external_excerpt: Businesses must retain packaging calculation records after submission.
internal_excerpt: Age-restricted products should be grouped into controlled lists with restricted user access.
rationale: The external source concerns packaging evidence retention; the internal source concerns product list access.

external_excerpt: Packaging submissions must be completed by the applicable reporting deadline.
internal_excerpt: The nightly integration schedule should complete before store assortment updates are released.
rationale: The external deadline is a regulatory packaging submission deadline; the internal passage is about system integration timing.

external_excerpt: A commercial organisation must have procedures designed to prevent bribery by persons associated with it.
internal_excerpt: Supplier onboarding can proceed without anti-bribery checks for agents or intermediaries.
rationale: The external obligation requires procedures for associated persons, while the internal wording permits bypassing checks for agents or intermediaries.

external_excerpt: A person is associated with a commercial organisation if they perform services for or on behalf of that organisation.
internal_excerpt: Anti-bribery due diligence only applies to direct employees, not to consultants or service providers.
rationale: The external source includes service providers as associated persons, while the internal wording excludes them.

external_excerpt: A person must not offer a financial advantage intending to induce another person to perform a function improperly.
internal_excerpt: Small facilitation payments may be offered to speed up routine approvals where the amount is low.
rationale: The internal wording permits a payment intended to influence performance that the external source prohibits.

external_excerpt: Organisations should communicate anti-bribery policies and training proportionately to those who perform services for them.
internal_excerpt: No training evidence is required for third parties after contract approval.
rationale: The external guidance expects communication and training for service providers, while the internal passage removes that evidence requirement.

external_excerpt: A commercial organisation must have procedures designed to prevent bribery by persons associated with it.
internal_excerpt: The onboarding procedure requires proportionate anti-bribery due diligence for agents, consultants and other associated persons.
rationale: Both passages require anti-bribery procedures or due diligence for associated persons.

external_excerpt: Procedures should manage gifts, hospitality and promotional expenditure where bribery risk could arise.
internal_excerpt: Gifts and hospitality above the low-value threshold must be approved and recorded before they are offered or accepted.
rationale: The internal control supports the external expectation to manage gifts and hospitality risk.

external_excerpt: Anti-bribery policies should be communicated and training should be provided proportionately to risk.
internal_excerpt: High-risk roles and third-party managers must complete annual anti-bribery training and policy acknowledgement.
rationale: The internal passage implements proportionate communication and training for higher-risk roles.

external_excerpt: Procedures should include clear reporting channels for bribery concerns and suspected improper conduct.
internal_excerpt: Employees and suppliers must report suspected bribery through the ethics channel or to the compliance owner.
rationale: Both passages require a clear route for reporting bribery concerns.

external_excerpt: A commercial organisation must have procedures designed to prevent bribery by persons associated with it.
internal_excerpt: Invoices should be matched to purchase orders before payment is released.
rationale: The external passage concerns anti-bribery procedures, while the internal passage concerns invoice matching.

external_excerpt: Organisations should assess bribery risks in markets, transactions and business relationships.
internal_excerpt: Product labels should include the approved customer-facing description and barcode.
rationale: The external passage is about bribery risk assessment, while the internal passage is about product label data.

external_excerpt: A person must not offer a financial advantage intending to induce improper performance.
internal_excerpt: The nightly interface should complete before store assortment updates are released.
rationale: The external passage prohibits bribery, while the internal passage concerns system integration timing.

external_excerpt: Procedures should include monitoring and review of anti-bribery controls.
internal_excerpt: Seasonal stock lists can be limited to authorised merchandising profiles.
rationale: The external passage is about monitoring anti-bribery controls, while the internal passage is about stock-list access.

external_excerpt: Organisations must respond to subject access requests without undue delay and within one month.
internal_excerpt: Subject access responses may be held until the next quarterly data governance meeting.
rationale: The external passage requires timely response within one month, while the internal passage permits delaying responses until a quarterly meeting.

external_excerpt: Personal data must be erased when it is no longer necessary for the purpose for which it was collected.
internal_excerpt: Customer personal data may be retained indefinitely after account closure for possible future analytics.
rationale: The external passage requires erasure once purpose ends, while the internal passage permits indefinite retention for possible future use.

external_excerpt: A personal data breach likely to risk individuals must be reported to the supervisory authority within 72 hours.
internal_excerpt: Privacy incidents can be logged internally and do not need regulatory reporting.
rationale: The external passage requires regulatory reporting for risky breaches, while the internal passage removes that reporting route.

external_excerpt: People must be able to withdraw consent as easily as they gave it.
internal_excerpt: Once marketing consent is captured, users cannot remove it through self-service channels.
rationale: The external passage requires easy withdrawal, while the internal passage blocks self-service withdrawal after consent is captured.

external_excerpt: Organisations must respond to subject access requests without undue delay and within one month.
internal_excerpt: Subject access requests must be logged immediately and answered within one calendar month unless a documented extension is approved.
rationale: The internal passage implements the one-month response expectation and adds an extension control.

external_excerpt: Personal data must be erased when it is no longer necessary for the purpose for which it was collected.
internal_excerpt: Customer personal data must be deleted or anonymised when the approved business purpose ends.
rationale: Both passages require data removal or anonymisation once the approved purpose no longer applies.

external_excerpt: A personal data breach likely to risk individuals must be reported to the supervisory authority within 72 hours.
internal_excerpt: Risky personal data breaches must be escalated to the data protection owner and reported to the regulator within 72 hours.
rationale: The internal passage mirrors the risky-breach reporting requirement and deadline.

external_excerpt: People must be able to withdraw consent as easily as they gave it.
internal_excerpt: Customers can withdraw marketing consent through the same preference centre used to give consent.
rationale: The internal passage supports the same ease-of-withdrawal expectation.

external_excerpt: Organisations must respond to subject access requests without undue delay and within one month.
internal_excerpt: Packaging dimensions and material weights should be recorded before the article is released to stores.
rationale: The external passage concerns data-subject access requests, while the internal passage concerns packaging data setup.

external_excerpt: A personal data breach likely to risk individuals must be reported to the supervisory authority within 72 hours.
internal_excerpt: Failed product-price integration jobs should retry every fifteen minutes until the nightly processing window closes.
rationale: The external passage concerns privacy breach reporting, while the internal passage concerns retry timing for system integrations.

external_excerpt: People must be able to withdraw consent as easily as they gave it.
internal_excerpt: Multiple commercial contracts may be needed when supplier fulfilment rules differ by operating model.
rationale: The external passage concerns consent withdrawal, while the internal passage concerns supplier contract structure.

external_excerpt: Personal data must be erased when it is no longer necessary for the purpose for which it was collected.
internal_excerpt: Article category hierarchy should match the approved commercial reporting structure before launch.
rationale: The external passage concerns personal-data erasure, while the internal passage concerns article category hierarchy.
